'Ndrina Tegano.description: The 'Ndrina De Stefano is a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, historically linked to the Tegano and Tripodo families. Originating in the 1950s under Domenico Tripodo, it became dominant after winning the first 'Ndrangheta war in the 1970s. The clan is based in the Archi district and has been involved in drug trafficking, economic infiltration, and alliances with Sicilian mafia and Camorra.

Detroit Partnership.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Detroit, Michigan, founded around 1908 by Pietro Mirabile. It operates primarily in the Greater Detroit area, with additional territories in Ohio, Ontario, and Las Vegas. The family engaged in bootlegging, labor racketeering, heroin trafficking, and casino skimming. Membership peaked at about 100 made men in the 1960s, declining to 40-50 by 2011, but remains active.

Nuova Mala del Brenta.description: The Nuova Mala del Brenta is a mafia-type criminal organization based in the Veneto region of Italy, believed to have emerged in the late 1990s as a successor to the original Mala del Brenta. It is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes, with a structure akin to Cosa Nostra and Camorra. The group is considered by Italian authorities to meet the legal definition of a mafia organization under Article 416 bis-cp.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta members are believed to operate as associates of Nuova Mala del Brenta.
edges.evidence_quote: Its members are exclusively native to the Veneto region, but with a number of associates operating in the region from Cosa Nostra, Camorra and it is believed from the Ndrangheta as well.

Messina Brothers.description: The Messina Brothers were five Maltese brothers who led a criminal organization in London from the 1930s to the 1950s, primarily involved in prostitution. They were based in Soho, London, and operated a network of brothels, importing women from Europe. The organization was founded by their father Giuseppe Messina in the early 1900s in Malta and Egypt, and the brothers expanded operations in London after being expelled from Egypt in 1932. They were eventually prosecuted and disbanded in the 1950s.

Rinaldi clan.description: The Rinaldi clan is a Camorra clan based in San Giovanni a Teduccio, Naples, founded in 1989 from a split of the Mazzarella clan. It is heavily involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, racketeering, and counterfeiting. Since 2019, it has formed a powerful alliance with the De Luca Bossa and Aprea-Cuccaro clans, becoming the dominant Camorra group in eastern Naples. The clan is allied with the Secondigliano Alliance and remains active.

Honeymoon Gang.description: The Honeymoon Gang was a mid-19th century New York street gang based in Manhattan's East Side 18th Ward. Södertälje mafia.description: Syriac-Aramean criminal organization based in Södertälje, Sweden, founded in the early 1980s. It is structured around family and kinship with no single leader, and has been involved in drug trafficking, murder, welfare fraud, and extortion. The organization has infiltrated local politics and welfare sectors, and was confirmed as a criminal network by the Svea Court of Appeal in 2014. As of 2019, it continues to grow in power.

Guardiões do Estado.description: Brazilian criminal faction operating in the state of Ceará, founded around 2017 in the Conjunto Palmeiras community.
